title: Fish and chickpea stew
description: Perfect — here’s a **one-pot tomato, chickpea & fish rice stew** you can make start to finish in the same pan:
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Perfect — here’s a **one-pot tomato, chickpea & fish rice stew** you can make start to finish in the same pan:

---

### 📝 Ingredients (serves 3–4)

- ~1 lb pollock (or other white fish), cut into 2-inch pieces  
- 1 can (15 oz) chickpeas, drained and rinsed  
- 1 can (14–15 oz) crushed tomatoes  
- 1 small onion, diced  
- 2–3 garlic cloves, minced  
- 2 tbsp olive oil  
- 1 cup uncooked long-grain rice (white or basmati cooks best in one pot)  
- 2½ cups water or broth (chicken or veggie)  
- 1 tsp smoked paprika (or regular)  
- ½ tsp cumin (optional but good)  
- Salt & pepper  
- Pinch of chili flakes or cayenne (optional)  
- ½ lemon or a splash of vinegar to finish  
- Fresh parsley/cilantro if you have it  

---

### 👩‍🍳 Method  

1. **Sauté base:**  
   In a deep skillet or Dutch oven, heat olive oil over medium heat. Sauté onion until soft (3–4 min). Add garlic and cook another 30 sec.

2. **Add spices:**  
   Stir in paprika, cumin, chili flakes, salt and pepper. Let them toast 30 sec.

3. **Add rice & chickpeas:**  
   Pour in the uncooked rice and chickpeas. Stir for 1 min so rice is coated in oil/spices.

4. **Add tomatoes & liquid:**  
   Stir in crushed tomatoes and the broth/water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a gentle simmer. Cover and cook 12–15 min, stirring once or twice, until the rice is mostly tender and liquid mostly absorbed.

5. **Add fish:**  
   Nestle pollock pieces on top of the rice mixture. Spoon a little sauce over the fish, cover again, and cook another 5–8 min until the fish is opaque and flakes easily and rice is fully done. (Add a splash more water if it looks dry.)

6. **Finish:**  
   Squeeze lemon juice over everything, adjust salt/pepper, and sprinkle with fresh herbs.

---

### 🍽 Tips  
- If you only have brown rice, start it separately or cook it longer with more liquid (it needs ~35–40 min before adding fish).  
- You can also stir in a handful of spinach or frozen peas at the end for extra color/veg.  

Would you like me to give you an **even faster “sheet pan” version** (fish + chickpeas + tomatoes roasted together in the oven)?

title: One-pan creamy fish recipe
description: Absolutely — this is *very* oven-friendly, and honestly even more hands-off (which feels right for winter cooking). The goal is the same cozy creaminess, just baked instead of babysat.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Absolutely — this is *very* oven-friendly, and honestly even more hands-off (which feels right for winter cooking). The goal is the same cozy creaminess, just baked instead of babysat.

## Oven-Baked Creamy Lemon-Garlic Cod (or Halibut)

**Best vessel:** oven-safe skillet *or* shallow baking dish  
**Oven temp:** 400°F / 205°C  
**Total time:** ~20–25 minutes

---

### Ingredients (same as before, with 2 small tweaks)
- 1–1.25 lb cod or halibut fillets  
- Salt & black pepper  
- 1 tbsp olive oil or butter  
- 3 cloves garlic, minced  
- ½ tsp paprika  
- ½ tsp dried thyme or Italian seasoning  
- ½ cup fish or vegetable broth  
- ¾ cup heavy cream or half-and-half  
- Zest of ½ lemon + juice to taste  
- 1–2 cups spinach **or** thinly sliced leeks  
- Optional: 2–3 tbsp grated parmesan  

---

### Oven Method (super simple)
1. **Preheat oven** to 400°F.  
2. Lightly oil an oven-safe skillet or baking dish.  
3. Season fish with salt & pepper and lay it in the dish.  
4. In a bowl, whisk together garlic, paprika, thyme, broth, cream, lemon zest, and parmesan (if using).  
5. Scatter spinach or leeks around (and slightly under) the fish.  
6. Pour the cream mixture over everything.  
7. Cover loosely with foil and bake **12–15 minutes**.  
8. Uncover and bake another **5–8 minutes**, until fish flakes easily and sauce thickens slightly.  
9. Finish with lemon juice, cracked pepper, and a drizzle of olive oil or knob of butter.

---

### Key oven tweaks (why they matter)
- **Cover first, uncover later** → keeps fish tender, then lets sauce concentrate  
- **Slightly thicker cream ratio** → ovens don’t reduce liquid like stovetops  
- **Leeks shine here** → they soften beautifully without sautéing  

---

### Optional broiler finish (restaurant-y touch)
If you want a little color:
- Sprinkle extra parmesan or breadcrumbs  
- Broil **1–2 minutes max** — keep a close eye on it

---

### What to watch out for
- Don’t overbake — cod is done at ~130–135°F internal  
- Halibut can go closer to 140°F but dries out fast past that  
- If sauce looks thin right out of the oven, rest it **5 minutes** — it thickens as it cools

---

### Make-ahead friendly?
Yes:
- Assemble everything up to 12 hours ahead  
- Cover + refrigerate  
- Add **3–5 extra minutes** to bake time

If you want, I can:
- Adjust this for a **sheet-pan version**
- Make it **dairy-free but still creamy**
- Add **potatoes or beans** so it’s truly one-and-done

Just tell me the direction 👍

title: One-Pan Halibut Skillet
description: Absolutely—this is a cozy, low-effort **one-pan halibut + brown rice skillet** that feels a little fancy but is secretly weeknight-easy. Minimal chopping, no weird ingredients, and great leftovers vibes.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Absolutely—this is a cozy, low-effort **one-pan halibut + brown rice skillet** that feels a little fancy but is secretly weeknight-easy. Minimal chopping, no weird ingredients, and great leftovers vibes.

---

## One-Pan Halibut with Leeky Brown Rice 🍋🐟

**Serves:** 2  
**Time:** ~40 minutes  
**Pan:** Oven-safe skillet or shallow Dutch oven with a lid (or foil)

### Ingredients
- 2 halibut fillets (5–6 oz each)
- 1 cup **brown rice**  
  - *Shortcut:* use **parboiled or microwave-ready brown rice** for true one-pan ease
- 1 medium **leek**, thinly sliced (white + light green parts)
- 2¼ cups **vegetable or chicken broth**
- 2 tbsp **olive oil**
- 2 cloves **garlic**, minced
- ½ tsp **salt** (more to taste)
- ¼ tsp **black pepper**
- ½ tsp **smoked paprika** or regular paprika
- Optional but great:
  - Zest of ½ lemon
  - Splash of white wine
  - Fresh parsley or dill
  - Small pat of butter

---

### Instructions

#### 1. Start the rice base
- Heat oven to **375°F (190°C)**.
- In your skillet over medium heat, add olive oil.
- Sauté leeks with a pinch of salt for **5–6 minutes** until soft and silky.
- Add garlic, cook **30 seconds**.
- Stir in rice, paprika, and black pepper. Toast **1 minute**.

#### 2. Simmer
- Add broth (and wine if using), stir, bring to a gentle simmer.
- Cover, transfer to oven, and bake **25 minutes**  
  *(until rice is mostly tender and liquid absorbed).*

#### 3. Add the halibut
- Season fillets with salt, pepper, and lemon zest.
- Nestle fish into the rice.
- Drizzle with a little olive oil (or dot rice with butter).
- Cover and return to oven for **10–12 minutes**, until halibut flakes easily.

#### 4. Finish
- Uncover for last **2–3 minutes** if you want a little texture on top.
- Sprinkle with herbs and a squeeze of lemon.

---

### Easy Variations
- **Creamy version:** Stir 2–3 tbsp crème fraîche or Greek yogurt into the rice at the end.
- **Mediterranean:** Add capers + olives with the fish.
- **Spicy:** Sprinkle chili flakes or a pinch of Aleppo pepper over the halibut.
- **Extra veg:** Toss in frozen peas or spinach right before adding the fish.

---

### One-Pan Reality Check (quick tip)
Classic brown rice can take longer than fish—so if you’re using *regular* brown rice and want zero stress:
- Par-cook the rice for 10 minutes first **or**
- Use parboiled / quick-cook brown rice

Both keep this firmly in **“easy weeknight”** territory.

If you want, I can tweak this for **lemon-dijon**, **miso-ginger**, or a **cozier winter-cream version** next.

title: Easy Miso Butter Cod
description: Oh hell yes — miso butter + cod is a weeknight power couple. White miso is *perfect* for this: mild, a little sweet, super umami. This comes together in ~20 minutes and feels way fancier than it is.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Oh hell yes — miso butter + cod is a weeknight power couple. White miso is *perfect* for this: mild, a little sweet, super umami. This comes together in ~20 minutes and feels way fancier than it is.

## Easy Miso Butter Cod (serves 2)

### Ingredients
- 2 cod fillets (about 5–6 oz each)
- 2 tbsp **unsalted butter**, softened  
- 1½ tbsp **white miso**
- 1 tsp **soy sauce** (or tamari)
- 1 tsp **honey or maple syrup**
- 1 small clove **garlic**, grated (optional but great)
- Black pepper
- Optional garnish: scallions, sesame seeds, squeeze of lemon

---

### Quick Miso Butter
In a small bowl, mix together:
- Butter  
- White miso  
- Soy sauce  
- Honey  
- Garlic  

You want a smooth, spreadable paste.

---

### Method (Oven + Broiler = Best Texture)

1. **Heat oven to 425°F (220°C).**
2. Pat cod dry and place on a foil- or parchment-lined sheet pan.
3. Lightly pepper the fish (no salt — miso’s salty).
4. Spread a generous layer of miso butter over the top of each fillet.
5. **Bake 10–12 minutes**, until cod flakes easily.
6. Switch to **broil for 1–2 minutes** to caramelize the top (watch closely!).

---

### Optional Easy Upgrades
- **Ginger twist:** add ½ tsp grated fresh ginger to the butter  
- **Extra richness:** finish with a tiny pat of plain butter on top  
- **Crispy edge:** roast on a preheated sheet pan for more browning  
- **Pan-sear version:** sear cod in oil 2–3 min per side, then add miso butter and baste

---

### What to Serve It With
- Steamed rice or coconut rice  
- Sautéed bok choy, spinach, or green beans  
- Roasted broccoli or asparagus  
- Cauliflower rice if you want it lighter

If you want, I can:
- Turn this into a **one-pan veggie + cod** situation  
- Adjust for **air fryer**  
- Scale it up for meal prep  
- Make it **slightly spicy** (gochujang lovers 👀)

Just say the word.

title: Creamy Cod with Leeks
description: Absolutely—this is *perfect* winter comfort food. Cozy, one-pan, and very forgiving.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Absolutely—this is *perfect* winter comfort food. Cozy, one-pan, and very forgiving.

## 🍲 Creamy Cod with Leeks & Mushrooms (Weeknight-Friendly)

### Why this works
- Leeks + mushrooms = deep, savory, cold-weather vibes  
- Cod stays tender while soaking up a creamy, herby sauce  
- Done in ~30 minutes, all in one pan

---

### Ingredients (2 servings)
**Main**
- 2 cod fillets (about 5–6 oz each)
- 2 leeks, white & light green parts only, sliced
- 6–8 oz mushrooms, sliced (cremini or button)

**Staples**
- 2 tbsp olive oil or butter (or a mix)
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- ½ cup broth (veg or chicken)
- ½ cup cream (heavy cream or half-and-half)
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional but great)
- ½ tsp dried thyme (or fresh if you have it)
- Salt & black pepper
- Lemon (optional, for finishing)

---

### Instructions
1. **Prep the cod**  
   Pat dry and season both sides with salt and pepper.

2. **Sear the cod**  
   Heat 1 tbsp oil/butter in a large skillet over medium-high.  
   Sear cod 2–3 minutes per side until lightly golden (it does *not* need to be cooked through).  
   Remove to a plate.

3. **Cook the veggies**  
   Lower heat to medium. Add remaining oil/butter.  
   Add leeks + mushrooms with a pinch of salt.  
   Cook 6–8 minutes until soft, lightly caramelized, and cozy-looking.

4. **Build the sauce**  
   Add garlic and thyme; cook 30 seconds.  
   Deglaze with broth, scraping up browned bits.  
   Stir in cream + Dijon. Simmer 2–3 minutes until slightly thickened.

5. **Finish the cod**  
   Nestle cod back into the skillet.  
   Simmer gently 4–6 minutes until cod flakes easily and sauce coats the back of a spoon.

6. **Finish & serve**  
   Taste for salt/pepper.  
   Add a small squeeze of lemon if you want brightness.

---

### How to serve it
- Over **mashed potatoes** or **polenta** (elite winter move)
- With **crusty bread** to mop up the sauce
- Alongside **farro or rice**
- Add frozen peas or spinach at the end for extra green

---

### Easy variations
- **Extra cozy:** swap half the cream for crème fraîche  
- **Lighter:** use all broth + a splash of milk  
- **More depth:** add ¼ cup white wine before the broth  
- **Protein boost:** stir in a can of cannellini beans at the end

If you want, I can:
- Convert this to a **baked/oven version**
- Make it **dairy-free**
- Or dial it toward **French bistro** vs **Nordic winter stew** vibes

## 🍲 Creamy Cod with Leeks & Mushrooms (Weeknight-Friendly)

### Why this works
- Leeks + mushrooms = deep, savory, cold-weather vibes  
- Cod stays tender while soaking up a creamy, herby sauce  
- Done in ~30 minutes, all in one pan

---

### Ingredients (2 servings)
**Main**
- 2 cod fillets (about 5–6 oz each)
- 2 leeks, white & light green parts only, sliced
- 6–8 oz mushrooms, sliced (cremini or button)

**Staples**
- 2 tbsp olive oil or butter (or a mix)
- 2 cloves garlic, minced
- ½ cup broth (veg or chicken)
- ½ cup cream (heavy cream or half-and-half)
- 1 tsp Dijon mustard (optional but great)
- ½ tsp dried thyme (or fresh if you have it)
- Salt & black pepper
- Lemon (optional, for finishing)

---

### Instructions
1. **Prep the cod**  
   Pat dry and season both sides with salt and pepper.

2. **Sear the cod**  
   Heat 1 tbsp oil/butter in a large skillet over medium-high.  
   Sear cod 2–3 minutes per side until lightly golden (it does *not* need to be cooked through).  
   Remove to a plate.

3. **Cook the veggies**  
   Lower heat to medium. Add remaining oil/butter.  
   Add leeks + mushrooms with a pinch of salt.  
   Cook 6–8 minutes until soft, lightly caramelized, and cozy-looking.

4. **Build the sauce**  
   Add garlic and thyme; cook 30 seconds.  
   Deglaze with broth, scraping up browned bits.  
   Stir in cream + Dijon. Simmer 2–3 minutes until slightly thickened.

5. **Finish the cod**  
   Nestle cod back into the skillet.  
   Simmer gently 4–6 minutes until cod flakes easily and sauce coats the back of a spoon.

6. **Finish & serve**  
   Taste for salt/pepper.  
   Add a small squeeze of lemon if you want brightness.

---

### How to serve it
- Over **mashed potatoes** or **polenta** (elite winter move)
- With **crusty bread** to mop up the sauce
- Alongside **farro or rice**
- Add frozen peas or spinach at the end for extra green

---

### Easy variations
- **Extra cozy:** swap half the cream for crème fraîche  
- **Lighter:** use all broth + a splash of milk  
- **More depth:** add ¼ cup white wine before the broth  
- **Protein boost:** stir in a can of cannellini beans at the end

- Convert this to a **baked/oven version**

Here’s the **full recipe (ingredients + instructions)** from the *Sweet As Honey* article for **Protein Pancakes Without Protein Powder** — scanned directly from the site: 

---

## 🥞 **Protein Pancakes Without Protein Powder**  
**Servings:** 2 (3 pancakes per serving)  
**Prep:** 15 min • **Cook:** 15 min • **Total:** 30 min 

### **Ingredients**
(All measurements in US Customary)

- ½ cup Greek Yogurt  
- ½ cup Milk of choice  
- 2 large Eggs  
- 1 ¼ cup Old-Fashioned Oats  
- 1 tablespoon Granulated Sweetener (e.g., sugar, allulose, etc.)  
- 2 teaspoons Baking Powder  
- ¼ teaspoon Salt  
- 1 teaspoon Vanilla Extract (optional) 

---

### **Instructions**

1. **Blend the batter:**  
   In a high-speed blender, add all ingredients — eggs, milk, Greek yogurt, oats, sweetener, baking powder, salt, and vanilla (if using). 

2. **Puree until smooth:**  
   Blend on high speed until the batter is liquid and smooth. 

3. **Rest the batter:**  
   Transfer the batter to a mixing bowl and let it rest for about **10 minutes** — this thickens it as the oats absorb liquid. 

4. **Heat the pan:**  
   Warm a pancake griddle or non-stick skillet over **medium heat**. Grease lightly with cooking spray or oil. 

5. **Cook the pancakes:**  
   Pour about **¼ cup** of batter per pancake onto the hot surface. Cook **2–3 minutes** until bubbles form on top. 

6. **Flip and finish:**  
   Flip and cook **another 1 minute** on the second side until golden. 

7. **Serve:**  
   Serve 3 pancakes per person. Top with your favorite syrup, fresh fruit, and/or extra yogurt. 

---

### **Storage**
- **Fridge:** Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 3 days.  
- **Freezer:** Freeze in zip-lock bags for up to 1 month (thaw in fridge overnight). 

---

If you’d like, I can also summarize the *nutrition info* or suggest topping ideas!

title: Healthy Dump-and-Bake Recipes
description: Absolutely—**dump-and-bake is my love language** 😄
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Absolutely—**dump-and-bake is my love language** 😄  
Here are **3 vegetarian, low-effort, high-protein-ish** recipes where you basically throw everything in a dish, bake, and walk away. No sautéing, no babysitting.

---

## 🥦 1. Dump-and-Bake Broccoli, Chickpea & Feta Quinoa Bake
**Protein boost:** quinoa + chickpeas + feta  
**Vibe:** cozy, Mediterranean, great leftovers

### Ingredients
- 1 cup **dry quinoa**, rinsed
- 1 can **chickpeas**, drained & rinsed
- 2 cups **broccoli florets** (fresh or frozen)
- ½ cup **crumbled feta**
- 2½ cups **vegetable broth**
- 2 tbsp **olive oil**
- 1 tsp **garlic powder**
- 1 tsp **smoked paprika**
- Salt & pepper

### Instructions
1. Heat oven to **375°F (190°C)**.
2. Dump *everything* into a 9×13 baking dish. Stir well.
3. Cover tightly with foil.
4. Bake **40 minutes**, uncover, stir.
5. Bake uncovered **10–15 more minutes** until liquid is absorbed.
6. Optional: squeeze of lemon or drizzle of olive oil.

**Protein:** ~20–22g per serving  
(Boost it further by serving with Greek yogurt or adding extra feta.)

---

## 🍅 2. Dump-and-Bake White Bean, Spinach & Tomato Casserole
**Protein boost:** white beans + mozzarella  
**Vibe:** Italian comfort, very weeknight-friendly

### Ingredients
- 2 cans **cannellini or butter beans**, drained
- 1 can **crushed tomatoes**
- 2 cups **baby spinach** (no chopping)
- 1 cup **shredded mozzarella** (or part-skim)
- 2 tbsp **olive oil**
- 1 tsp **Italian seasoning**
- ½ tsp **red pepper flakes** (optional)
- Salt & pepper

### Instructions
1. Oven to **375°F**.
2. Dump everything into a baking dish and gently mix.
3. Bake uncovered **30–35 minutes** until bubbly.
4. Optional: broil 2–3 minutes for a golden top.

**Protein:** ~22–25g per serving  
Serve with whole-grain toast or spoon over farro.

---

## 🌶️ 3. Dump-and-Bake Red Lentil Coconut Curry (Ultra Lazy Edition)
**Protein boost:** red lentils  
**Vibe:** warming, protein-dense, no stirring required

### Ingredients
- 1 cup **dry red lentils**, rinsed
- 1 can **light coconut milk**
- 1½ cups **vegetable broth**
- 1 cup **frozen cauliflower or sweet potato cubes**
- 1 tbsp **curry powder**
- ½ tsp **turmeric**
- Salt to taste

### Instructions
1. Oven to **375°F**.
2. Dump all ingredients into a baking dish and stir.
3. Cover tightly with foil.
4. Bake **45–50 minutes**, stir once halfway if you remember.
5. Uncover last 10 minutes if you want it thicker.

**Protein:** ~23–26g per serving  
Excellent over brown rice or eaten straight from a bowl.

---

### Want to optimize further?
I can:
- Push these to **30–35g protein** per serving  
- Make them **higher fiber / lower calorie**
- Convert one into a **sheet-pan version**
- Customize for **meal prep (4–5 days fridge-friendly)**

Just tell me your priority 👌

title: 1️⃣ Crispy Miso-Maple Tofu with Garlicky Greens
description: 1. **Mix sauce:** miso, maple syrup, soy sauce, vinegar.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

**Savory, lightly sweet, very weekend-y**

### Ingredients
- 1 block extra-firm tofu, pressed & cubed
- 1½ tbsp white miso
- 1 tbsp maple syrup (or honey)
- 1 tbsp soy sauce or tamari
- 1 tsp rice vinegar or lemon juice
- 1–2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 cloves garlic, sliced
- Optional: spinach, kale, or green beans

### How
1. **Mix sauce:** miso, maple syrup, soy sauce, vinegar.
2. Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high. Pan-fry tofu 8–10 min until golden on most sides.
3. Lower heat, add garlic, cook 30 seconds.
4. Add sauce + splash of water. Toss until glossy and caramelized.
5. Toss in greens until just wilted.

**Serve with:** rice, quinoa, or over soba noodles  
**Optional upgrade:** sprinkle sesame seeds or chili crisp

---

description: 1. Pan-sear tofu in oil until golden; remove and set aside.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

**Comfort-food vibes, great for a slow Sunday**

### Ingredients
- 1 block extra-firm tofu, sliced
- 1½ tbsp white miso
- ½ cup coconut milk or regular milk/cream
- 1 tsp grated ginger (or ground ginger)
- 1 clove garlic, minced
- 1 tbsp soy sauce
- 1 tbsp oil

### How
1. Pan-sear tofu in oil until golden; remove and set aside.
2. Lower heat. Add garlic + ginger, cook 30 seconds.
3. Whisk miso with coconut milk + soy sauce, add to pan.
4. Simmer gently 3–5 min until slightly thick.
5. Return tofu, spoon sauce over, warm through.

**Serve with:** rice, mashed potatoes, or sautéed mushrooms  
**Optional:** add scallions or black pepper on top

title: Low-Carb Sides for Eggs
description: Perfect—here’s a **5-day, pescatarian, low-carb, low-effort breakfast rotation** that hits **≥30g protein every day** and is easy to autopilot on busy mornings. I kept everything to one pan or microwave + assembly. 🙌
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Perfect—here’s a **5-day, pescatarian, low-carb, low-effort breakfast rotation** that hits **≥30g protein every day** and is easy to autopilot on busy mornings. I kept everything to one pan or microwave + assembly. 🙌

> **Macro note:** calories/protein are approximate and assume low-fat cottage cheese + nonfat Greek yogurt. You can swap brands and tweak portions easily.

---

## 🗓 5-Day High-Protein Egg Breakfast Rotation (Pescatarian)

### **Monday – Spinach Cottage Cheese Power Scramble**  
**~330–360 cal | ~34–38g protein**  
**How:**  
- 2 eggs  
- ¾ cup liquid egg whites  
- Big handful spinach  
- ½ cup low-fat cottage cheese  
- Salt, pepper, chili flakes  

⏱️ 5 min | 🧼 1 pan

---

### **Tuesday – Smoked Salmon Brunch Bowl**  
**~350–390 cal | ~32–38g protein**  
**How:**  
- 2 eggs  
- ½ cup egg whites  
- 2–3 oz smoked salmon  
- Optional: ¼ cup cottage cheese or dollop nonfat Greek yogurt  
- Lemon + dill + black pepper  

⏱️ 5 min | 🧼 1 pan + plate

---

### **Wednesday – Mushroom Parm Protein Scramble**  
**~300–340 cal | ~30–34g protein**  
**How:**  
- 2 eggs  
- ¾ cup egg whites  
- Sautéed mushrooms  
- 2–3 tbsp parmesan  
- Garlic powder + black pepper  

⏱️ 5 min | 🧼 1 pan

---

### **Thursday – Kimchi Cottage Cheese Scramble**  
**~320–360 cal | ~32–36g protein**  
**How:**  
- 2 eggs  
- ½–¾ cup egg whites  
- ½ cup low-fat cottage cheese  
- 2–3 tbsp kimchi  
- Optional: ½ tsp sesame oil  

⏱️ 4 min | 🧼 1 pan

---

### **Friday – Avocado Salsa Protein Plate**  
**~340–380 cal | ~30–33g protein**  
**How:**  
- 2 eggs  
- ¾ cup egg whites  
- ¼–½ avocado  
- Salsa or pico  
- Optional: feta sprinkle  

⏱️ 5 min | 🧼 1 pan + plate

---

## ⚡ Ultra-Busy Backup (2 minutes, microwave)
Use this whenever mornings go sideways:
- Microwave **2 eggs + ¾ cup egg whites**  
- Eat **½ cup cottage cheese on the side**  
**→ ~30–35g protein**

---

## 🛒 Weekly Grocery List (set it and forget it)
**Protein base**
- Eggs  
- Liquid egg whites  
- Low-fat cottage cheese  
- Nonfat Greek yogurt (optional)  
- Smoked salmon  

**Veg + flavor**
- Spinach  
- Mushrooms  
- Kimchi  
- Salsa / pico  
- Avocados  
- Parmesan or feta  
- Lemon, dill, chili flakes  

---

## 🔁 How to keep this from getting boring
- Rotate **spices**: smoked paprika, za’atar, everything bagel seasoning  
- Rotate **heat**: chili crisp, sriracha, harissa  
- Rotate **herbs**: dill (salmon days), scallions (kimchi days)

---

## 🎯 Fat-Loss Optimization (no misery)
- Keep **1–2 whole eggs**, add whites for protein  
- Use **low-fat cottage cheese / nonfat Greek yogurt**  
- Limit avocado to **¼ fruit** most days  
- Go heavy on **veg + acid + spice** for satisfaction

---

If you want, I can also build you a **high-protein, low-carb lunch rotation** that pairs well with this so your whole day supports your summer cut without feeling restrictive.

Oh I *definitely* clocked the high-protein breakfast arc 😄 Oatmeal can be a sneaky protein bomb without touching protein powder. Here are some **high-protein, low-to-moderate calorie oatmeal ideas** using real foods (all pescatarian-friendly):

---

## 🥣 High-Protein Oatmeal Base (No Powder)
Start with this for any variation:
- **½ cup rolled oats**
- **1 cup soy milk** (or ultra-filtered milk if you do dairy)  
- **½ cup egg whites** (stir in at the end over low heat)  
👉 ~25–30g protein just from the base

**How to avoid “egg white oatmeal” vibes:**  
Whisk egg whites with a splash of milk first, then slowly stir in off heat. It makes the oats creamy, not eggy.

---

## 🍓 1. Cheesecake-Style Berry Oatmeal (Sweet, High Protein)
**Protein: ~30–35g | Calories: ~350–400**

- Oats + soy milk base  
- Stir in:
  - **½ cup low-fat cottage cheese** (blends in creamy)
  - **½ tsp vanilla**
  - **Frozen berries**
  - **Cinnamon**
- Optional: 1 tsp maple syrup or honey

👉 Tastes like berry cheesecake oats, super filling

---

## 🍌 2. Banana PB “Power Oats”
**Protein: ~25–30g | Calories: ~400–450**

- Oats + soy milk base  
- Stir in:
  - **2 tbsp powdered peanut butter (PB2)**  
  - **½ banana, mashed**
  - **Cinnamon + pinch of salt**

👉 More protein, less fat than regular peanut butter  
👉 If you want extra protein: swirl in **Greek yogurt** at the end

---

## 🍎 3. Apple Pie Protein Oatmeal
**Protein: ~28–32g | Calories: ~350–400**

- Cook oats with:
  - **Diced apple**
  - **Cinnamon, nutmeg**
- Finish with:
  - **½ cup Greek yogurt** (or skyr)
  - **1 tbsp hemp seeds**

👉 Creamy + crunchy + dessert vibes

---

## 🥥 4. Tropical High-Protein Oats (No Powder, No Dairy Option)
**Protein: ~22–26g**

- Oats cooked in **soy milk**
- Stir in:
  - **Chia seeds**
  - **Hemp seeds**
  - **Coconut flakes**
  - **Mango or pineapple**

👉 Lower calorie than nut butters  
👉 Good fiber + omega-3 combo

---

## 🧂 5. Savory Miso-Egg White Oatmeal (Trust Me)
**Protein: ~30–35g | Calories: ~350–400**

If you like savory breakfasts:

- Cook oats in **water or broth**
- Stir in:
  - **Egg whites**
  - **1 tsp white miso**
  - **Scallions**
  - **Sesame seeds**
- Optional: soft-boiled egg or tofu cubes

👉 Feels like congee / savory rice bowl  
👉 Insanely filling without being heavy

---

## ⚡ Highest Protein Combo (Best Macro Hack)
If your goal is **max protein, minimal calories**, this combo is elite:

**Oats + Soy Milk + Egg Whites + Greek Yogurt**  
→ **35–40g protein** for ~400 calories  
→ Very hard to beat for fullness per calorie

---

## Grocery List (High-ROI Adds)
If you want to “protein-optimize” oatmeal long-term:
- Soy milk or ultra-filtered milk  
- Liquid egg whites  
- Greek yogurt / skyr  
- Cottage cheese  
- Hemp seeds  
- PB2 (powdered peanut butter)

---

I can build you a **Mon–Fri oatmeal lineup** that:
- Hits 25–40g protein daily  
- Stays under ~400–450 calories  
- Keeps flavors totally different so you don’t get bored

title: Gochujang Tofu Recipe
description: Oh yeah—making this a **gochujang tofu “steak”** is a great move. Fewer cuts, more drama on the plate, and it actually stays juicier inside. Here’s the tweaked version with **two thick slabs** instead of cubes 👇
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Oh yeah—making this a **gochujang tofu “steak”** is a great move. Fewer cuts, more drama on the plate, and it actually stays juicier inside. Here’s the tweaked version with **two thick slabs** instead of cubes 👇

---

## 🔥 Gochujang Tofu Steaks (20-Minute Skillet, Minimal Prep)

**Serves 2**  
**Protein:** ~35–40g total

### Ingredients
- 1 block extra-firm tofu (14–16 oz), pressed  
- 1 tbsp olive oil  
- 2 cloves garlic, minced  
- 1 tsp grated ginger (or ½ tsp ground)  
- 2 tbsp gochujang  
- 1 tbsp low-sodium soy sauce or tamari  
- 1 tbsp rice vinegar or lime juice  
- 1 tsp maple syrup or honey  
- 1 tsp sesame oil (optional but 🔥)  
- 3–4 tbsp water (a little extra since steaks need more sauce)  
- Optional: chili crisp or flakes  
- Optional garnish: scallions, sesame seeds (skip if allergic)

---

### How To Make It

1. **Slice the tofu into steaks**  
   Press tofu well, then slice the block **in half horizontally** so you get two thick slabs.

2. **Sear for crispy edges**  
   Heat olive oil in a wide skillet over medium-high.  
   Add tofu slabs and cook 4–5 min per side until deeply golden and lightly crisped.

3. **Build aromatics**  
   Push tofu to the side. Add garlic + ginger to the pan.  
   Sauté ~30 seconds until fragrant.

4. **Glaze with gochujang sauce**  
   Whisk sauce ingredients together.  
   Pour into pan, spoon sauce over tofu, and let simmer 2–3 minutes.  
   Flip once and spoon more sauce on top until glossy and sticky.

5. **Finish & rest**  
   Kill the heat and let tofu sit in the sauce for 30–60 seconds so it soaks in flavor.

---

## 🍽 How to Serve (Fast + Delicious)

**Weeknight bowl**
- Serve tofu steak over rice or farro  
- Add cucumber, quick-pickled carrots, or sautéed greens

**Low-carb plate**
- Tofu + garlicky spinach or roasted broccoli  
- Spoon extra sauce over veggies

**Wrap it**
- Slice tofu steak and stuff into lettuce wraps or a warm tortilla with slaw

---

## 🔁 Optional Flavor Tweaks

**Extra Crispy Exterior**
- Dust tofu lightly with cornstarch before searing (optional, still quick)

**Smoky Gochujang**
- Add ½ tsp smoked paprika or a few drops of liquid smoke

**Sweet-Heat BBQ-ish Vibe**
- Add 1 tsp ketchup + ½ tsp maple to the sauce

---

## ⚡ Ultra-Low-Effort Version (No Pan Babysitting)
If you want *zero flipping*:

- Brush tofu steaks with oil  
- Bake at **425°F (220°C) for 20–25 min**, flipping once  
- Warm sauce in pan or microwave, then glaze tofu at the end

---

If you want, I can also give you:
- a **sheet-pan version with broccoli + tofu steaks**, or  
- a **gochujang-miso glaze** that’s a little deeper and umami-heavy.

title: Savory Miso Oatmeal Recipe
description: Here’s a **detailed version of the savory miso-broth oatmeal** we discussed. It’s one of the easiest **high-protein breakfasts without protein powder**, and it fits well with your **pescatarian + low-carb-leaning breakfast goals**. It also
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Here’s a **detailed version of the savory miso-broth oatmeal** we discussed. It’s one of the easiest **high-protein breakfasts without protein powder**, and it fits well with your **pescatarian + low-carb-leaning breakfast goals**. It also comes together in **~7 minutes** and uses ingredients you often mention having (miso, soy sauce, eggs).

---

## High-Protein Savory Miso Broth Oatmeal

### Nutrition (approx.)
- **Calories:** ~380–420  
- **Protein:** ~30–33 g  
- **Carbs:** ~30 g  
- **Fat:** ~12 g  

*(Great balance if you’re trying to cut a bit before summer but still want a filling breakfast.)*

---

# Ingredients (1 serving)

**Base**
- ½ cup **rolled oats**
- ¾ cup **vegetable broth** (or dashi if you have it)
- ¼ cup **unsweetened soy milk**
- 1 tsp **white or yellow miso paste**

**Protein**
- ¾ cup **liquid egg whites** (or whites from ~3 eggs)
- 1 large **whole egg**

**Umami & seasoning**
- 1 tsp **soy sauce**
- ½ tsp **sesame oil** *(optional but great flavor)*
- Black pepper

**Toppings (optional but recommended)**
- 1 tbsp **scallions**
- 1 tbsp **kimchi** *(optional but excellent)*
- ½ sheet **nori**, shredded
- Chili crisp or chili flakes

---

# Step-by-Step Instructions

### 1. Cook the oats in broth
In a small saucepan combine:

- ½ cup oats  
- ¾ cup broth  
- ¼ cup soy milk  

Bring to a **gentle simmer** over medium heat.

Cook **3–4 minutes**, stirring occasionally.

The broth adds umami so the oatmeal tastes more like **savory congee or ramen base**.

---

### 2. Add the miso (important step)
Reduce heat to **low**.

In a small bowl:
- Mix **1 tsp miso** with **1–2 tbsp hot broth from the pot**.

Stir this mixture back into the oats.

This prevents miso from clumping and preserves its flavor.

---

### 3. Add the egg whites (protein boost)
Slowly pour in **¾ cup egg whites** while stirring constantly.

Cook **1–2 minutes**.

They will **thicken the oats and turn them creamy**, almost like risotto.

---

### 4. Cook the whole egg
You have two good options:

**Option A (easiest):**
- Crack the egg directly into the oats.
- Cover and cook **1–2 minutes** until just set.

**Option B (my favorite):**
- Soft fry the egg separately in a small pan with **½ tsp olive oil**.
- Place it on top of the oatmeal.

---

### 5. Finish the bowl
Stir in:
- 1 tsp soy sauce  
- black pepper  
- sesame oil (optional)

Top with:
- scallions  
- kimchi  
- nori  
- chili crisp

Break the yolk and mix it in — it makes the oats **extra creamy and rich**.

---

# Why this works (nutritionally)
Protein sources:

| Ingredient | Protein |
|---|---|
Oats | ~5 g |
Soy milk | ~3 g |
Egg whites | ~20 g |
Whole egg | ~6 g |

**Total:** ~34 g protein

This keeps you full much longer than standard oatmeal.

---

# Super Fast Version (5 minutes)

If you're busy in the morning:

1. Microwave oats + broth + soy milk **2 minutes**
2. Stir in miso  
3. Stir in egg whites  
4. Microwave **1 more minute**
5. Top with a soft egg or just mix another egg white in

Still **~30 g protein**.

---

💡 **Two optional upgrades I think you'd like based on your past recipes:**

1. **Miso-Mushroom Oatmeal** – sauté mushrooms first in olive oil, then add oats  
2. **Shrimp Miso Oatmeal** – add 3–4 quick sautéed shrimp on top (adds ~10g protein)

---

If you'd like, I can also show you **3 more savory high-protein oatmeal variations** that are excellent for rotation:

- **Kimchi-tofu oatmeal (very high protein)**
- **Mediterranean feta & spinach oatmeal**
- **Miso-mushroom oatmeal that tastes like ramen broth**.

title: Bake the fish
description: Heat oven to **400°F (205°C)**.

Heat oven to **400°F (205°C)**.

Place fish in a baking dish:

- drizzle with olive oil  
- salt + pepper  

Bake **10–12 minutes** until flaky.

*(Great method for thicker cod or sablefish.)*

---

Whisk:

- Greek yogurt  
- lemon juice  
- garlic  

Slowly whisk in warm water until **smooth and pourable**.

Plate like this:

3. Drizzle **lemon tahini sauce** generously  

Finish with herbs, chili flakes, and lemon zest.

Roast cherry tomatoes with olive oil for 15 minutes and add to the plate. The acidity balances the tahini perfectly.

title: Crispy Oven-Roasted Brussels Sprouts
description: Here’s a **simple, reliable way to make Brussels sprouts in the oven** that turns them crispy, caramelized, and slightly sweet without adding many calories. This method is very forgiving and works great with weeknight dinners.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Here’s a **simple, reliable way to make Brussels sprouts in the oven** that turns them crispy, caramelized, and slightly sweet without adding many calories. This method is very forgiving and works great with weeknight dinners.

---

## Crispy Oven-Roasted Brussels Sprouts

### Ingredients (2–3 servings)
- 1 lb Brussels sprouts  
- 1–1½ tbsp olive oil  
- ½ tsp kosher salt  
- ¼ tsp black pepper  
- Optional finish: squeeze of lemon **or** 1 tsp balsamic vinegar

### Instructions

1. **Preheat oven**
   - Set oven to **425°F (220°C)**.

2. **Prep the sprouts**
   - Trim the tough stem ends.
   - Cut each sprout **in half lengthwise**.

3. **Season**
   - Toss sprouts with:
     - olive oil  
     - salt  
     - pepper  

4. **Arrange for crispiness**
   - Place **cut side down** on a sheet pan.
   - Spread them out so they **aren’t crowded** (this is the key to browning).

5. **Roast**
   - Bake for **18–25 minutes**.

title: Shrimp and Black Bean Stew
description: Great idea—both of these build on the same shrimp + black bean base but give you **two very different textures**:
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Great idea—both of these build on the same shrimp + black bean base but give you **two very different textures**:  
- a **thick chili-style stew**  
- a **one-pot cauliflower rice protein bowl**

Both stay aligned with your usual goals: **high protein, reasonable calories, pescatarian, and easy weeknight cooking**.

---

# 1. Thick Shrimp & Black Bean Chili

This version is **heartier and thicker**—almost like a chili. Great if you want something comforting but still pretty light.

### Ingredients (2 servings)

- 10 oz shrimp (peeled & deveined)  
- 1 cup black beans (rinsed)  
- 1 small onion, diced  
- 2 cloves garlic, minced  
- 1 tbsp olive oil  
- 1 tsp cumin  
- 1 tsp chili powder  
- ½ tsp smoked paprika  
- 1 cup crushed tomatoes  
- ¾ cup vegetable broth  
- ½ red bell pepper, diced  
- 1 tbsp tomato paste  
- juice of ½ lime  

Optional toppings  
- avocado  
- cilantro  
- Greek yogurt or sour cream

---

### Instructions

**1. Cook aromatics**

Heat olive oil over medium heat.

Add:
- onion  
- bell pepper  

Cook **5 minutes** until softened.

Add garlic, cumin, paprika, chili powder. Cook **30 seconds**.

---

**2. Build the chili**

Add:

- tomato paste  
- crushed tomatoes  
- broth  
- black beans  

Simmer **12–15 minutes**. The stew should **reduce and thicken**.

---

**3. Add shrimp last**

Add shrimp and cook **3–4 minutes** until just opaque.

Finish with lime juice.

---

### Nutrition (approx)

Per serving:

- Calories: ~380  
- Protein: ~38g  
- Carbs: ~30g  
- Fat: ~12g

---

# 2. One-Pot Shrimp, Black Bean & Cauliflower Rice Stew

This version is **lighter and more meal-prep friendly**. The cauliflower rice absorbs the broth and makes it almost like a **shrimp jambalaya or risotto vibe**.

### Ingredients (2 servings)

- 10 oz shrimp  
- 2 cups cauliflower rice (frozen is fine)  
- 1 cup black beans  
- 1 small onion diced  
- 2 cloves garlic  
- 1 tbsp olive oil  
- 1 tsp cumin  
- ½ tsp smoked paprika  
- 1 cup diced tomatoes  
- ¾ cup vegetable broth  
- juice of ½ lime  
- handful cilantro

Optional flavor boosters

- ½ tsp oregano  
- dash hot sauce  
- 1 tsp soy sauce (adds umami)

---

### Instructions

**1. Cook aromatics**

Heat olive oil in a deep skillet.

Add onion and cook **4 minutes**.

Add garlic, cumin, paprika.

---

**2. Build the base**

Add:

- tomatoes  
- broth  
- black beans  

Simmer **5 minutes**.

---

**3. Add cauliflower rice**

Stir in cauliflower rice and cook **5–6 minutes**.

The mixture will **thicken as the rice absorbs broth**.

---

**4. Add shrimp**

Add shrimp and cook **3 minutes** until pink.

Finish with lime juice and cilantro.

---

### Nutrition (approx)

Per serving:

- Calories: ~330  
- Protein: ~40g  
- Carbs: ~20g  
- Fat: ~10g

Very close to the **high-protein / lower-carb dinners you’ve been experimenting with recently.**

---

💡 **Quick flavor trick for either recipe**

Add **½ tsp cocoa powder or a square of dark chocolate** to the chili version.  
It’s a classic trick used in Mexican chili that adds **depth without sweetness**.

---

✅ Since you cook shrimp and cauliflower rice pretty often, I can also show you **3 more shrimp + bean dishes that feel restaurant-level but stay under ~450 calories** (one of them is a *Spanish shrimp & white bean stew that’s incredible).

title: Creamy Greek Yogurt & Dill Fish Stew
description: Greek yogurt gives the stew a **luxuriously creamy texture** without the fat of cream, while cauliflower rice thickens the broth.
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

*(Bright, tangy, Mediterranean-style — great with cod, halibut, or sablefish)*

### Why this works
Greek yogurt gives the stew a **luxuriously creamy texture** without the fat of cream, while cauliflower rice thickens the broth.

### Ingredients (2 servings)
- 10–12 oz **cod, halibut, or sablefish**, cut into chunks  
- 1 ½ cups **frozen cauliflower rice**
- 1 tbsp **olive oil**
- 1 small **leek**, thinly sliced
- 2 cloves **garlic**, minced
- 1 cup **low-sodium vegetable or fish broth**
- ½ cup **plain Greek yogurt**
- zest + juice of **½ lemon**
- 1 tbsp **fresh dill** (or 1 tsp dried)
- ½ tsp **paprika**
- salt + pepper
- optional: handful **spinach or kale**

### Instructions
1. **Sauté aromatics**
   - Heat olive oil in a medium pot.
   - Cook leek with a pinch of salt for **4–5 minutes** until soft.
   - Add garlic and paprika and cook **30 seconds**.

2. **Build the stew**
   - Add broth and cauliflower rice.
   - Simmer **5 minutes** so the cauliflower softens and slightly thickens the liquid.

3. **Cook the fish**
   - Add fish pieces.
   - Simmer gently **4–6 minutes** until just cooked.

4. **Temper the yogurt**
   - In a bowl mix yogurt with lemon zest and juice.
   - Stir in **2–3 tablespoons hot broth** to warm it.

5. **Finish**
   - Reduce heat to low.
   - Stir yogurt mixture into stew.
   - Add dill and greens if using.

### Estimated nutrition (per serving)
- ~420 calories  
- ~40 g protein  
- ~18 g fat  
- very low carb

---

### Ingredients (2 servings)
- 10–12 oz **cod, halibut, or shrimp**
- 1 ½ cups **frozen cauliflower rice**
- 1 tbsp **olive oil**
- 1 shallot or small onion, diced
- 2 cloves **garlic**
- 1 tsp **fresh grated ginger**
- 1 tsp **red curry paste**
- 1 cup **light coconut milk**
- 1 cup **vegetable or seafood broth**
- 1 tbsp **soy sauce or fish sauce**
- juice of **½ lime**
- handful **cilantro**
- optional heat: sliced chili or chili crisp

### Instructions
1. **Sauté aromatics**
   - Heat oil in a pot.
   - Cook shallot **3–4 minutes**.
   - Add garlic, ginger, curry paste and cook **1 minute**.

2. **Build broth**
   - Add coconut milk and broth.
   - Bring to a gentle simmer.

3. **Add cauliflower rice**
   - Stir in cauliflower rice.
   - Simmer **5 minutes** so it thickens slightly.

4. **Cook fish**
   - Add fish pieces.
   - Simmer **4–5 minutes** until just cooked.

5. **Finish**
   - Stir in soy sauce, lime juice and cilantro.

- ~450 calories per serving  
- ~35–40 g protein  
- very filling because of cauliflower rice

- Add **miso paste** (you already use this a lot) to deepen flavor

- Top with **herbs + chili oil**

title: Ricotta Protein Breakfast Ideas
description: Ricotta is a great ingredient for **high-protein sweet breakfasts** because it’s creamy, naturally a little sweet, and pairs well with egg whites, oats, or yogurt to push the protein higher. Since you liked the **ricotta crepes** before, I’
ingredients:
TO REVIEW: extracted from ChatGPT export

Ricotta is a great ingredient for **high-protein sweet breakfasts** because it’s creamy, naturally a little sweet, and pairs well with egg whites, oats, or yogurt to push the protein higher. Since you liked the **ricotta crepes** before, I’ll give you a couple of similar options that keep that **dessert-like feel but stay fairly macro-friendly**.

*(All are nut-free to fit your allergy and pescatarian preferences.)*

---

# 1. Lemon Ricotta Protein Pancakes 🍋
Fluffy, slightly tangy pancakes that taste indulgent but are packed with protein.

### Ingredients (2 servings)
- ½ cup ricotta  
- 2 eggs  
- ½ cup egg whites  
- ⅓ cup oat flour (or blended oats)  
- 1 tsp baking powder  
- Zest of ½ lemon  
- ½ tsp vanilla  
- Pinch salt  
- Optional sweetener: 1–2 tsp honey or maple syrup

**Topping**
- ½ cup Greek yogurt  
- Berries  
- Drizzle honey

### Instructions
1. Whisk ricotta, eggs, egg whites, vanilla, and lemon zest.
2. Stir in oat flour, baking powder, and salt.
3. Let batter rest **3–4 minutes** to thicken.
4. Cook pancakes in a lightly oiled skillet on medium heat (~2 minutes per side).
5. Serve with Greek yogurt and berries.

### Approx macros (per serving)
- **Protein:** ~32 g  
- **Calories:** ~340  
- **Very filling** thanks to egg whites + ricotta.

---

# 2. Whipped Ricotta Breakfast Bowl (Dessert-Style) 🍓
This one feels like **cheesecake mousse** but is surprisingly macro-friendly.

### Ingredients (1 large serving)
- ½ cup ricotta  
- ½ cup Greek yogurt  
- ¼ cup egg whites *(optional but boosts protein)*  
- ¼ tsp vanilla  
- 1 tsp honey or maple syrup  
- Cinnamon pinch

**Toppings**
- Berries  
- 2 tbsp toasted oats or granola substitute  
- Lemon zest

### Instructions
1. If using egg whites: microwave them **30–40 sec** until just set and cool slightly.
2. Blend or whisk ricotta + yogurt + vanilla + honey until **light and fluffy**.
3. Fold in the cooked egg whites (they disappear into the mixture).
4. Top with berries and oats.

### Approx macros
- **Protein:** ~35 g  
- **Calories:** ~320–350

Very similar satisfaction to **cheesecake filling**.

---

# 3. Ricotta-Stuffed French Toast 🍞
This one feels like a brunch dish but can still stay fairly lean.

### Ingredients (2 servings)
- 3 slices high-protein bread  
- 2 eggs  
- ¼ cup egg whites  
- Cinnamon + vanilla

**Filling**
- ½ cup ricotta  
- ¼ cup Greek yogurt  
- 1 tsp honey  
- Lemon zest

### Instructions
1. Mix ricotta, yogurt, honey, and lemon zest.
2. Make sandwiches with bread + ricotta filling.
3. Whisk eggs, egg whites, cinnamon, vanilla.
4. Dip sandwiches and cook in skillet until golden.

### Approx macros (per serving)
- **Protein:** ~30–35 g  
- **Calories:** ~360–400

---

💡 **Quick Ricotta Trick (I think you’ll like this):**  
If you **blend ricotta + Greek yogurt**, it becomes **very similar to cheesecake filling**. It works as a topping for:

- oatmeal  
- crepes  
- pancakes  
- baked sweet potatoes  
- waffles

…and adds **15–25g protein instantly**.

---

✅ **If you'd like, I can also show you:**
- **3 ultra-high protein ricotta breakfasts (40–50g protein)**  
- **A ricotta crepe upgrade** that tastes like **cannoli filling but is still macro-friendly**.
